steps.title: Read the slope from the table
steps.content: The table gives: $x = 1 \Rightarrow g(1) = 34$, $x = 2 \Rightarrow g(2) = 31$, $x = 3 \Rightarrow g(3) = 28$, $x = 4 \Rightarrow g(4) = 25$. Each time $x$ increases by 1, $g(x)$ decreases by 3, so the slope is $m = -3$.

steps.title: Solve for b
steps.content:
Use any table row in $g(x) = -3x + b$. With $(x, g(x)) = (1, 34)$:
$$34 = -3(1) + b$$
$$b = 34 + 3 = 37$$
The answer is 37.
